title: artemisia campestris subsp. maritima
intro: Artemisia campestris subsp. maritima, also known as sea wormwood, is a subspecies of the artemisia campestris plant, a perennial herb native to the coastal regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of sea wormwood include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Sea wormwood can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a range of herbal preparations.
